design_philosophy.core_loop: World map exploration via raft navigation → island entry → side-scrolling platforming with equipment collection → boss defeat → progression to new islands. Equipment and items unlock access to previously inaccessible areas.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Non-linear exploration with gated progression: players must discover which items/equipment unlock which areas, balancing freedom with guided advancement.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Adventure Island explorer seeking lost memories while navigating a mysterious kingdom, discovering secrets and acquiring power through exploration and equipment.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately departed from previous linear Adventure Island entries by embracing Metroidvania-style exploration and character progression. Emphasizes non-linear level design, equipment-gated access, and world interconnectedness over pure platforming execution. Targets 'younger and first-time adventurers' with accessible gameplay while maintaining action depth.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Equipment system provides scaling difficulty: different armor, shields, spells, and weapons modify player capability. Exploration-based progression allows players to sequence content and build power before facing challenges.

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-based: weapons, armor, shields, spells, and equipment pieces discovered across islands. Each item/equipment has gating properties controlling access to new areas.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Equipment-gated: specific items and equipment open previously inaccessible passages and areas on multiple islands. Encourages backtracking and non-linear exploration.
replayability_hooks: Hidden areas and extended map sections (e.g., extended tree foliage in Poka-Poka Island boss chamber, snow-layered replica chamber in Hiya-Hiya Island)
Multiple equipment configurations enabling different playstyles
Non-linear progression path allows varied sequence of island exploration
Character progression system encourages multiple playthroughs with different equipment setups

world_building_techniques: Amnesia premise creating mystery motivation for exploration
Kingdom setting with NPCs (king, kidnapped girl) establishing stakes
Environmental storytelling through island discovery and character encounters
Stage completion cutscenes featuring character interactions (Tina's face imagery in completion scenes)

boss_design.philosophy: Sequential progression through island bosses with distinct encounter chambers. Boss rooms contain hidden extensions and layered design suggesting iterative development or future content planning.
notable_bosses: Poka-Poka Island boss (with extended upward map extension)
Hiya-Hiya Island mammoth boss (with right-side chamber replica)
Phantom (appears in debug menu as final boss)
Space Demon/Scorpion (appears in debug menu as second final boss)

faction_indicators: Mysterious girl with amnesia (potential ally/quest giver)
danger_signals: Storm separation event establishing external threat
Girl's mysterious kidnapping indicating hostile force
Amnesia as narrative device suggesting memory loss from danger
environmental_storytelling: Multi-island archipelago structure suggesting exploration-based discovery
Raft-based world navigation implying maritime setting and isolation
Equipment variety (weapons, armor, spells) suggests diverse island ecosystems and challenges
Inn and King's chambers as central hub locations (Waku-Waku Island) establishing social structures

sources_used: tcrf - Super Adventure Island II (The Cutting Room Floor)
